title: Fix unreachable TP math for near-1.0 entries

context.problem_statement: Tonight's approved audit found that near-1.0 entries can compute a take-profit above 1.0 on a 0-1 contract. Example: trade #259 BUY_NO at entry 0.9447 generated TP 1.3226, which is unreachable.
context.manager_intent: Implement the smallest safe fix so TP logic cannot produce unreachable targets for near-resolution entries.

objective: Ensure TP cannot exceed the meaningful upper bound for 0-1 contracts, while preserving normal TP behavior for ordinary entries.
conditional_allowed_files: C:\Users\johnny\Desktop\sports_bot_v2\.env only if the task brief requires using an existing configured near-resolution threshold and a tiny env adjustment is truly necessary
forbidden_actions: Read every file in the repo
required_work: Inspect the current TP computation path in core/risk.py
Patch the smallest safe logic so TP cannot exceed a meaningful upper cap for 0-1 contracts
Preferred direction from approved audit: cap TP at min(tp_price, NEAR_RESOLUTION_PRICE) if that fits the current architecture cleanly
If a different minimal cap is safer within the existing code structure, document it clearly
Preserve normal TP behavior for non-extreme entries
acceptance_criteria: TP targets cannot exceed the chosen upper cap for 0-1 contracts
Normal entries still compute TP normally below the cap
No files outside allowed scope are modified
verification_required: Explain old behavior vs new behavior
Run py_compile on each changed Python file
State whether restart is required for the fix to take effect
